question: How does the existing Stripe integration handle refunds, chargebacks, and disputes? I need: (1) which Stripe webhook events are currently handled in api/handlers/webhook.go, (2) how FinancialTransaction records reversals, (3) how the existing promotions/discounts system in promotions.go works (relevant because coupons interact with refunds), (4) for a Stripe Connect marketplace where I take a commission on each course sale, what is the recommended way to handle: full refunds (reverse the transfer + refund the charge), partial refunds, and lost chargebacks. Walk through the webhook events I need to add and the FinancialTransaction updates required.
answer:
The existing Stripe integration in the [`lastsaas`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L1) repository handles refunds, chargebacks, and disputes primarily through Stripe webhook events processed in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go).

**1. Stripe Webhook Events Handled in `api/handlers/webhook.go`**

The [`WebhookHandler`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L30) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go) currently handles the following Stripe webhook events:
*   [`checkout.session.completed`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L414) (Citation: [`handleCheckoutCompleted`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L145) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go))
*   [`invoice.paid`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L415) (Citation: [`handleInvoicePaid`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L350) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go))
*   [`invoice.payment_failed`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L416) (Citation: [`handleInvoicePaymentFailed`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L450) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go))
*   [`customer.subscription.updated`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L417) (Citation: [`handleSubscriptionUpdated`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L523) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go))
*   [`customer.subscription.deleted`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L418) (Citation: [`handleSubscriptionDeleted`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L585) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go))
*   [`charge.refunded`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L136) (Citation: [`handleChargeRefunded`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L643) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go), [Refund and dispute handling](#lastsaas-version-notes-v12-e28094-march-1-2026))
*   [`charge.dispute.created`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L136) (Citation: [`handleDisputeCreated`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L689) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go), [Refund and dispute handling](#lastsaas-version-notes-v12-e28094-march-1-2026))
*   [`charge.dispute.closed`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L136) (Citation: [`handleDisputeClosed`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L735) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go), [Refund and dispute handling](#lastsaas-version-notes-v12-e28094-march-1-2026))

These are defined in the [`main.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L493) for the server, which sets up the webhook handler and the routes (Citation: [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/cmd/server/main.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go)). The [`README.md`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fmanifest.json#L17) also explicitly lists these as part of the Stripe setup (Citation: [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/README.md`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md)).

**2. How [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) Records Reversals**

The [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) model (defined in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/models/billing.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go)) records reversals by creating a new transaction of [`Type`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L24) [`TransactionRefund`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L25).

When a [`charge.refunded`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L136) webhook event is received (Citation: [`handleChargeRefunded`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L643) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go)):
*   The system records a [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) with [`Type`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L24) set to [`models.TransactionRefund`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L674).
*   The [`AmountCents`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L170) and [`SubtotalCents`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L35) fields are recorded as negative values ([`-refundedAmount`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L674)) to reflect the reversal.
*   The [`Description`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L27) is set to "Refund".
*   The [`StripeSessionID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L40) field is populated with the Stripe [`charge.ID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L674) associated with the refund.
*   An internal invoice number is generated using [`h.stripe.NextInvoiceNumber`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L784).

This approach ensures that refunds are explicitly captured as separate line items in the financial records, detailing the negative impact on revenue (Citation: [`recordTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L783) function in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go)).

**3. How the Existing Promotions/Discounts System in `promotions.go` Works**

The promotions/discounts system, primarily managed in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/promotions.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go), leverages Stripe's coupon and promotion code functionalities. This system allows administrators to:

*   **Create Coupons and Promotion Codes:** A new promotion involves creating a Stripe [`coupon`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L36) (specifying the discount type, e.g., [`percentOff`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L307) or [`amountOff`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L308), and optional product restrictions) and then linking it to a [`promotioncode`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go#L22) with a user-facing code, redemption limits, and an expiration date (Citation: [`CreatePromotion`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go#L243) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/promotions.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go)).
*   **Manage Discounts:** Discounts can be percentage-based or a fixed amount. The currency for fixed-amount discounts is configurable. (Citation: [`CreatePromotion`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go#L243) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/promotions.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go)).
*   **Set Redemption Limits and Expiry:** Promotion codes can have a [`MaxRedemptions`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go#L59) limit and an [`ExpiresAt`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftokens.go#L23) date (Citation: [`CreatePromotion`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go#L243) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/promotions.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go)).
*   **Restrict to Specific Products:** Promotions can be restricted to apply only to certain subscription [`plans`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fdb%2Fmongodb.go#L385) or [`credit bundles`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L118). The system maps internal [`Plan`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L37) and [`CreditBundle`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fcredit_bundle.go#L9) IDs to their corresponding Stripe Product IDs (Citation: [`buildProductNameMap`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go#L111), [`ListEligibleProducts`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go#L190), [`resolveStripeProducts`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go#L368) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/promotions.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go)).
*   **Update and Deactivate:** Administrators can update the [`name`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L77) of a coupon and activate/deactivate promotion codes (Citation: [`UpdatePromotion`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go#L446), [`DeactivatePromotion`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go#L492) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/promotions.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fpromotions.go)).
*   **Integration with Checkout:** Stripe Checkout sessions have [`AllowPromotionCodes: true`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L248) enabled (Citation: [`CreateCheckoutSession`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L183) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/stripe/stripe.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go)), allowing users to apply these codes during the purchase process.

The [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend/src/pages/admin/PromotionsPage.tsx`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fpages%2Fadmin%2FPromotionsPage.tsx) provides the UI for administrators to manage these promotions.

**Interaction with Refunds:** While coupons themselves don't directly "interact" with refunds in the sense of triggering them, they reduce the initial charge. When a refund occurs, the [`charge.refunded`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L136) event will reflect the amount actually refunded, which would be the discounted price if a coupon was applied. The [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) record for the refund will reflect this reduced amount.

**4. Stripe Connect Marketplace Refund and Chargeback Handling**

For a Stripe Connect marketplace where you take a commission on each course sale (using direct or destination charges with `on_behalf_of`), handling refunds and chargebacks requires additional webhook events and logic beyond the current LastSaaS implementation. The current system is designed for a direct SaaS billing model, not a marketplace.

Here's a recommended approach:

**Assumptions for a Stripe Connect Marketplace:**
*   **Platform (you):** The Connect account, receives commission.
*   **Sellers (course creators):** Connected accounts, receive the net amount after commission.
*   **Charge Type:** You are likely using `destination charges` (where you create the charge on your platform account and specify a `transfer_data` hash to transfer funds to the connected account, with an optional `application_fee_amount`) or [`direct charges`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L56) (where the connected account creates the charge and you apply an `application_fee`). The following assumes `destination charges` or [`direct charges`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L56) where your platform initiates the refund.

**Required Webhook Events to Add:**

To handle refunds and chargebacks effectively for a Connect marketplace, you would primarily need to add handlers for events related to transfers and disputes affecting connected accounts.

1.  **`transfer.reversed`**:
    *   **Purpose:** This event is crucial when you perform a refund that reverses an initial transfer to a connected account.
    *   **Action:** When you refund a charge that involved a `transfer_data` to a connected account, Stripe automatically reverses the associated transfer. This webhook informs your system that the funds transferred to the seller have been reclaimed by the platform.
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update:**
        *   Create a new [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) for the *seller's account* (connected account) with [`Type: TransactionRefund`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L265).
        *   Record the [`AmountCents`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L170) as negative, representing the funds debited from the seller.
        *   You might need a new [`TransactionType`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L20) like `TransactionConnectTransferReversal` or similar, to differentiate it from direct refunds to customers.
        *   Link this transaction to the original sale and the customer refund.

2.  **`charge.dispute.funds_withdrawn`**:
    *   **Purpose:** This event signifies that funds related to a disputed charge have been withdrawn from your account (or the connected account, depending on who bears the risk).
    *   **Action:** When a customer disputes a charge and the dispute progresses, Stripe may withdraw the disputed amount from the account that received the original funds.
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update:**
        *   Create a [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) with a new [`Type`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L24) like `TransactionDisputeWithdrawal`.
        *   Record the [`AmountCents`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L170) as negative, representing the funds lost due to the dispute.
        *   This transaction would be against the connected account if they are responsible for chargeback losses, or against your platform account if you are.

3.  **`charge.dispute.funds_reinstated`**:
    *   **Purpose:** If you win a dispute after funds were withdrawn, Stripe reinstates the funds.
    *   **Action:** This webhook indicates that previously withdrawn funds have been returned.
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update:**
        *   Create a [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) with a new [`Type`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L24) like `TransactionDisputeReinstatement`.
        *   Record the [`AmountCents`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L170) as positive, representing the recovered funds.
        *   This would reverse the `TransactionDisputeWithdrawal` or offset it.

**Walkthrough for Marketplace Scenarios:**

**A. Full Refunds (Reverse Transfer + Refund Charge)**

1.  **User Initiates Refund:** Your system receives a request to refund a course purchase.
2.  **Platform Action:** You call the Stripe API to refund the original charge. If the original charge included a `transfer_data` to a connected account, Stripe automatically creates a transfer reversal for the amount of the original transfer.
3.  **Webhook Event: [`charge.refunded`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L136) (Existing)**
    *   The [`handleChargeRefunded`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L643) function in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go) will process this.
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update:** It will record a [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) of [`Type: TransactionRefund`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L265) (or a similar, more specific type like `TransactionCustomerRefund`) for the customer's initial payment against the *platform's* revenue, with a negative [`amountCents`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L417). This accurately reflects the platform's portion of the refund.
    *   **Internal Actions:** Trigger any internal events ([`billing.refund_received`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L677)) and update the relevant course sale record in your database as refunded.
4.  **Webhook Event: `transfer.reversed` (NEW)**
    *   **Logic:** You would add a new handler function, e.g., `handleTransferReversed`, in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go).
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update:**
        *   Inside `handleTransferReversed`, identify the connected account (seller) from the `transfer.reversed` event.
        *   Record a new [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) with a new [`Type`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L24) (e.g., `TransactionConnectTransferReversal`) against the *seller's* ledger in your system (if you track connected account balances).
        *   This transaction's [`AmountCents`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L170) would be negative, indicating the funds taken back from the seller.
        *   Link this to the original sale and the customer refund.
    *   **Internal Actions:** Adjust the seller's balance or payout records in your system. Notify the seller of the refund and transfer reversal.

**B. Partial Refunds**

1.  **User Initiates Partial Refund:** Your system receives a request to partially refund a course purchase (e.g., $50 out of $100).
2.  **Platform Action:** You call the Stripe API to partially refund the original charge. If transfers were involved, Stripe will automatically adjust the associated transfers proportionally or you might need to manually create a partial transfer reversal if using `separate charges and transfers`.
3.  **Webhook Event: [`charge.refunded`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L136) (Existing)**
    *   The [`handleChargeRefunded`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L643) function will execute.
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update:** It will record a [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) of [`Type: TransactionRefund`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L265) for the partial amount against the platform's revenue, with a negative [`amountCents`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L417) matching the refunded amount.
    *   **Internal Actions:** Update the course sale record to reflect the partial refund.
4.  **Webhook Event: `transfer.reversed` (NEW - if applicable)**
    *   If Stripe automatically performed a partial transfer reversal, the `transfer.reversed` webhook will fire with the partial amount.
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update:** The `handleTransferReversed` function would record a `TransactionConnectTransferReversal` against the seller for the partial amount reclaimed.
    *   **Internal Actions:** Adjust the seller's balance proportionally.

**C. Lost Chargebacks**

1.  **Charge Disputed:** A customer disputes the original charge with their bank.
2.  **Webhook Event: [`charge.dispute.created`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L136) (Existing)**
    *   The [`handleDisputeCreated`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L689) function in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go) will be triggered.
    *   **Current Action:** It currently sets the tenant's [`billingStatus`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L87) to [`models.BillingStatusPastDue`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fadmin.go#L148) and logs a critical event.
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update (Enhancement):** You might consider creating a [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) with a new [`Type`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L24) (e.g., `TransactionDisputePending`) to temporarily hold the disputed amount, but this isn't strictly necessary until funds are lost.
    *   **Internal Actions:** Mark the course sale as disputed in your database. Notify the seller of the dispute, as they may be responsible for providing evidence.
3.  **Webhook Event: `charge.dispute.funds_withdrawn` (NEW)**
    *   **Logic:** Add a new handler function, `handleDisputeFundsWithdrawn`, in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go).
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update:**
        *   Identify whether the funds were withdrawn from the platform account or the connected account (this depends on your Connect setup and dispute liability).
        *   Record a [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) with `Type: TransactionDisputeWithdrawal` with a negative [`AmountCents`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L170) against the relevant account (platform or seller). This accurately reflects the loss.
    *   **Internal Actions:** If the seller bears the risk, debit their balance. Update the course sale record to reflect funds withdrawn. Notify the seller of the fund withdrawal.
4.  **Webhook Event: [`charge.dispute.closed`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L136) (Existing)**
    *   The [`handleDisputeClosed`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L735) function processes this event.
    *   **Current Action:** It logs the outcome and, if the dispute was won, sets the tenant's [`billingStatus`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L87) back to [`models.BillingStatusActive`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmetrics%2Fmetrics.go#L237).
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update (Enhancement):**
        *   If the dispute was **lost** ([`dispute.Status != "won"`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L53)), and funds were previously withdrawn (via `charge.dispute.funds_withdrawn`), no further [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) is strictly needed as the `TransactionDisputeWithdrawal` already accounted for the loss. However, you might want to finalize the `TransactionDisputePending` to a `TransactionDisputeLoss` if you implemented a pending state.
        *   If the dispute was **won**, and funds were withdrawn, you'd expect a `charge.dispute.funds_reinstated` event to follow.
5.  **Webhook Event: `charge.dispute.funds_reinstated` (NEW - if applicable)**
    *   **Logic:** Add a new handler function, `handleDisputeFundsReinstated`, in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go).
    *   **FinancialTransaction Update:**
        *   If funds were previously withdrawn (e.g., via `TransactionDisputeWithdrawal`), record a [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) with `Type: TransactionDisputeReinstatement` with a positive [`AmountCents`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L170) against the relevant account to credit the recovered funds.
    *   **Internal Actions:** Credit the seller's balance if they were debited. Update the course sale record to reflect the dispute win and fund reinstatement.

**Summary of [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29) Updates:**

You would likely extend the [`models.TransactionType`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L783) enum (Citation: [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/models/billing.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go)) and the [`recordTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go#L783) function in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/webhook.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fwebhook.go) to support these new types:

*   `TransactionCustomerRefund`: for refunds to the end-customer (platform-side).
*   `TransactionConnectTransferReversal`: for funds reclaimed from connected accounts during a refund.
*   `TransactionDisputeWithdrawal`: for funds lost due to a dispute.
*   `TransactionDisputeReinstatement`: for funds recovered after winning a dispute.

Each new type would record the appropriate amount (positive for income/recovery, negative for expenses/losses), link to the relevant Stripe IDs (charge, transfer, dispute), and identify the affected entity (platform or specific connected account) if applicable.
